decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the local hot spot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip, directly lowering the peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation.
